one_time_description: Boundary Claim per capita payment of $1,500 per member totaling $4,023,000 distributed to members
economic_notes: Fort William First Nation owns 100% of Anemki Mountain Business Trust (GBE). Holds 25% interest in Lot 251-3 Development LP via Holdings LP, and 50% interest in Wiigiiwaaganag LP. Advances made to Bamkushwada Limited Partnership of $1,500,000. Earned consulting/management revenues of $400,000 from Bamkushwada LP and $1,100,000 from East-West Tie Limited Partnership. Approximately 1,100 acres of land returned in 1999 subject to ongoing municipal property tax dispute with City of Thunder Bay ($11,842,866 disputed).
notes: Auditor firm name not present in extracted text. Consolidated entity includes 11 subsidiaries (trusts and corporations) and 1 GBE (Anemki Mountain Business Trust) accounted for by modified equity method. Two business partnerships (Lot 251-3 Development LP at 25% and Wiigiiwaaganag LP at 50%) also accounted for by modified equity method. Significant contingent liability of $11,842,866 in disputed municipal property taxes from City of Thunder Bay not recorded in financial statements. Restricted cash of approximately $50.2M relates to various trust accounts. Community and youth trust accounts of $97,097,410 are large investment portfolios. Boundary Claim per capita distributions of $1,500/member ($4,023,000) are recurring but significant. Total government transfers calculated as ISC + CMHC + all provincial ministry transfers. OSR includes other revenue, investment income, gain on sale of investments, and lottery fund revenue. Annual debt service reflects only principal repayments on long-term debt per cash flow statement.
